description: Validates whether accounts flagged in the population as settled in full (SIF) have sufficient corroborating evidence in account tags and AR activity logs. Produces a step-level evidence decision and a final account-level QC verdict.

agents.purpose: Retrieves structured population batches. Performs no QC judgment.
Gathers raw evidence for one account using registered evidence tools. Returns evidence only.
Evaluates evidence and rules to produce step-level or final-level QC decisions. Does not retrieve data.

population_phase.description: Retrieve the accounts to be reviewed. The orchestrator runs this phase once per batch before entering the account phase.

steps.title: Load population batch
steps.objective: Retrieve a paginated batch of accounts in scope for this QC run.

account_phase.description: For each account in the population batch, the orchestrator runs evidence collection, then step-level decisions, then the final account decision. Steps are driven by depends_on and step_type — the orchestrator must not hardcode a fixed sequence.

steps.title: Collect SIF tag evidence
steps.objective: Gather SIF account tag evidence for the account.
steps.checkpoint_note: In checkpoint scope, run for the first account only.

steps.title: Evaluate SIF tag evidence
steps.objective: Apply rule_sif_tag to the tag evidence collected in acct-1a. Return a step-level decision.
steps.orchestration_hint: Call make_qc_decision with decision_mode=step_decision. Pass account_context, evidence_bundle from acct-1a, and the rule subset for evaluation_rule_ids (rule_sif_tag only).

steps.title: Collect AR log settlement evidence
steps.objective: Gather AR log evidence for the account.

steps.title: Evaluate AR log settlement evidence
steps.objective: Apply rule_arlog_direct and conditional rule_arlog_comment to the AR log evidence.
steps.orchestration_hint: Call make_qc_decision with decision_mode=step_decision. Pass account_context, evidence_bundle from acct-2a, and the rule subset (rule_arlog_direct + rule_arlog_comment). Note rule_arlog_comment is conditional_fallback — skip if settled_in_full_found is true.

steps.title: Final account QC verdict
steps.objective: Aggregate step decisions from acct-1b and acct-2b into one final QC verdict.
steps.orchestration_hint: Call make_qc_decision with decision_mode=final_decision. Pass account_context, step_decisions=[acct-1b result, acct-2b result], and evaluation_rules for rule_final_aggregation.

evaluation_rules.title: SIF tag alignment
evaluation_rules.description: The account tag SIF presence must align with settlement_flag.

decision_matrix.note: Flag=Y and SIF tag present — consistent
Flag=Y but no SIF tag found — missing expected tag is a QC failure
Flag=N but SIF tag found — direct contradiction
Flag=N and no SIF tag — consistent

evaluation_rules.title: AR log direct settlement record
evaluation_rules.description: If settlement_flag=Y, settled_in_full_found must be true to pass. If settlement_flag=N and settled_in_full_found=true, that is a contradiction.

decision_matrix.note: Flag=Y and SIF AR log row found — consistent
Flag=Y but no SIF AR log row — missing expected evidence is a QC failure
Flag=N but SIF AR log row found — direct contradiction
Flag=N and no SIF AR log row — consistent

evaluation_rules.title: AR log comment secondary check
evaluation_rules.description: When no direct settled_in_full rows exist, the latest AR log comment is reviewed for secondary settlement implication.
evaluation_rules.fallback_condition: Only applies when settled_in_full_found is false in arlog_settlement_evidence. If settled_in_full_found is true, the AR log check is already satisfied by rule_arlog_direct — skip this rule entirely. A skipped fallback rule does NOT count as a failure. When latest_comment_message is null (no comment present), treat as does_not_imply_settlement — absence of a comment does not imply settlement.
evaluation_rules.comment_implies_settlement_when: The comment positively asserts the account was settled, paid in full, or fully resolved.
comment_does_not_imply_settlement_when: The settlement keyword is negated — e.g. 'not final settlement', 'no settlement activity', 'without settlement'
The comment is ambiguous or pending — e.g. 'awaiting confirmation', 'under review', 'pending'
The comment merely references a settlement-related topic without confirming it — e.g. 'references payoff review but not final settlement'

decision_matrix.note: Flag=Y and comment implies settlement — supports the flag
Flag=Y but AR log comment does not imply settlement — QC failure
Flag=N but comment implies settlement — contradiction
Flag=N and comment does not imply settlement — consistent

evaluation_rules.title: Final verdict aggregation
evaluation_rules.description: Aggregates step-level decisions into one final account verdict. Apply the aggregation policy defined in the prompt.

decision_policy.description: The orchestrator may call the decision agent dynamically whenever sufficient evidence exists for a step. Step decisions are not batched — they are triggered per account as soon as the required depends_on steps are complete. The final decision is triggered after all required step decisions for the account are complete.

result_schema.description: Defines the expected fields of each per-account QC result produced by the orchestrator. The orchestrator must populate all account_level_fields for every successfully processed account.

account_level_fields.description: The account identifier from the population phase.
Full account context carried from the population output. Must include all fields from the population step's required_output_fields: account_number, settlement_flag, borrower, co_borrower.
Keyed by step_id. Contains the raw evidence bundle returned by each evidence_collection step.
Keyed by step_id. Contains the decision string (pass / fail / manual_review / error) for each step_decision step. Does NOT include the final_decision step.
Keyed by step_id. Contains one concise reason string for each step_decision step.
Aggregated final verdict from the final_decision step. Allowed values: pass, fail, manual_review.
Concise plain-text explanation of the final verdict.

execution_status.note: manual_review must only appear inside final_decision or step_decisions. It must never be used as execution status. System status and business decision are always separate.

error_schema.description: Populated only when status is error. Never populated on a completed run.

fields.description: Short error class identifier (e.g. tool_failure, schema_error, timeout).
Human-readable explanation of what went wrong.
The step_id at which the failure occurred, if known. Omit if not applicable.

checkpoint_scope.description: Limits for local testing and flow validation. Not for production use.
checkpoint_scope.selection_rule: process the first account in the first batch only
